name: Las Tres Burbonas by the Bayas River — circular through the Gorbeia Natural Park
name_original: Las Tres Burbonas por el río Bayas — circular por el Parque Natural de Gorbeia
region_intro: Hiking around Zuya/Zuia offers diverse terrain within the historical territory of Álava in the Basque Country, Spain. The region is characterized by its significant portion of Gorbeia Natural Park, featuring varied landscapes from lush forests to mountain peaks. Notable natural features include the Peñas de Oro with their beech and oak woods, the scenic Altube River Waterfall, and extensive deciduous forests. This area provides a range of hiking experiences across its natural parks and varied elevations. Best hiking trails around Zuya/Zuia The most popular hiking route is Las Tres Burbonas by the Bayas River — circular through the Gorbeia Natural Park, a moderate 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 56 minutes to complete. This circular path explores the Gorbeia Natural Park along the Bayas River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gorbea Summit – Gorbea Summit loop from Gorbeialdea, a difficult 10.8 miles (17.4 km) path. This route involves a significant ascent to the Gorbea Summit, offering expansive views. Local hikers also love the Gorbea Summit – Gorbea Summit Cross loop from Zuia, a 12.3 miles (19.9 km) trail leading through mountain terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 31 minutes. Hiking around Zuya/Zuia is defined by Gorbeia Natural Park, diverse forests, and mountain peaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging summit ascents. The routes in Zuya/Zuia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 1,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Zuya/Zuia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Zuia?
A: Zuia offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 60 routes available. These range from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families in Zuia?
A: Yes, Zuia has several easy trails perfect for families. One excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14996946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita Jugatxi – Echevarría Bakery loop from Murgia&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 5.5 km long and takes around 1 hour 40 minutes. This route allows you to explore the woods and visit the historic Jugatxi hermitage.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Zuia?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Zuia are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056795454/las-tres-burbonas-by-the-bayas-river-circular-through-the-gorbeia-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tres Burbonas by the Bayas River — circular through the Gorbeia Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.5 km long and takes nearly 4 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any hikes in Zuia that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is home to the scenic Altube River Waterfall. You can explore this natural beauty on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10514213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altube River Waterfall – Corraladas Waterfall loop from Beluntza&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.5 km hike that takes about 2 hours 40 minutes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Zuia?
A: Hiking in Zuia offers diverse terrain. You'll find lush deciduous forests, particularly beech and oak woods, as well as mountain paths leading to peaks within the Gorbeia Natural Park. The Peñas de Oro also provide varied landscapes and panoramic views.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints to see while hiking in Zuia?
A: Zuia's trails offer access to several interesting landmarks and viewpoints. You can hike to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4136536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit&lt;/a&gt; for expansive views, or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Oro&lt;/a&gt;. The 13th-century Jugatxi Hermitage is also a cultural attraction nestled within the woods.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Zuia?
A: The best time to hike in Zuia is generally during spring, summer, and early autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery, especially when the deciduous forests are in full bloom or displaying their autumn colors. Winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear for colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Zuia?
A: Certainly. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Zuia offers demanding routes to the Gorbea Summit. Options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3991360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit – Gorbea Summit loop from Gorbeialdea&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 17.4 km trail, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10514357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit – Gorbea Summit Cross loop from Zuia&lt;/a&gt;, which is nearly 20 km long and involves significant ascent.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Zuia?
A: The hiking trails in Zuia are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the beauty of the Gorbeia Natural Park, and the variety of routes available for all skill levels.

Q: Is there an interpretation center for Gorbeia Natural Park near Zuia?
A: Yes, visitors can find the Gorbeia Natural Park's Interpretation Centre, known as Parketxea, in Sarría, a village close to Murgia. It offers valuable insights into the local environment and the park's unique features.

Q: Are there any huts or refuges available along the hiking trails in Zuia?
A: Yes, for those undertaking longer or more challenging hikes, there are several huts and refuges in the area. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3324587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6010414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isisibarri Cabin&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4136552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zenigorta Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, which can provide shelter or a place to rest.
